iteration_2.changes: Compressed skill, removed Python/TS duplication, added workflow guidance to write Python scripts and execute them, added explicit 'Do NOT use read_file' instruction

iteration_3.changes: Made 'Do NOT use read_file' even stronger with CRITICAL WORKFLOW section — this backfired, causing model to avoid file operations entirely

iteration_4.changes: Reverted to softer 'Recommended Approach' wording, kept concise Python-only skill with formulas and key rules

iteration_5_final.changes: No changes from iteration 4 — same skill, different random seed

changes_from_original: Removed duplicate TypeScript implementations (kept Python only since qwen3-30b performs better writing Python scripts)
Removed K-Nearest Neighbors section (unused by tasks)
Removed verbose mathematical notation in favor of concise code
Added 'Recommended Approach' section guiding model to write Python scripts that handle file I/O
Condensed Common Pitfalls to essential items only
Reduced skill size from ~150 lines to ~55 lines to save context window for small model
notes: qwen3-30b has a non-deterministic tool call formatting issue where it sometimes wraps parameters in a 'raw' field, causing tool failures. The skill optimization mitigates this by encouraging the write-script-then-execute pattern (which uses write_file + execute_command, both more resilient), but cannot fully eliminate the randomness. Both tasks achieved 100/100 on the final iteration.
